Clients

A client is a business or person that your organisation issues sales invoices to. The client list is the billing-side counterpart to the supplier list. Like suppliers, clients are party records — they can be contacted, given addresses, and have billing defaults attached to them.

What is a client

A client is a party record (a business or person) that has a client relationship attached to it. The client relationship stores accounts receivable defaults — the GL account invoices should be posted to, and the default payment term for calculating due dates. The same organisation can be both a supplier and a client simultaneously; Merlin handles this through separate relationship records on the same party.

Creating a client

  1. Go to Billing › Clients.
  2. Click New Client.
  3. Fill in the client details and optionally add a contact person.
  4. Click Save.

Client fields

FieldRequiredNotes
Legal nameYesThe full registered name; max 255 characters
Trading nameNoDisplay name if different from legal name
EmailNoMust be a valid email address
PhoneNo
NotesNoInternal notes
StatusYesOne of active, pending, or inactive
Billing addressNoAddress line, city, and other address fields used on invoices
Payment termNoDefault payment term applied when creating invoices for this client

Contacts

You can add a contact person to a client at the same time as creating the client. The contact form accepts:

FieldRequiredNotes
First nameYesMax 100 characters
Last nameNo
EmailNoMust be a valid email address; used for invoice delivery
PhoneNo

Contact people are used when selecting recipients for sales invoice emails. Multiple contacts can be added to a client after creation through the client detail page.

Default receivable account and payment term

Every client gets its own receivable account, created automatically as a child of the control account configured in Billing Settings — see Client receivable sub-accounts. The default payment term is also stored on the client's relationship record. When you create a sales invoice and select this client, the receivable account and payment term are pre-filled from these defaults. You can override both values on any individual invoice. If no control account is configured yet, or the client predates this feature, the system-level default from Billing Settings is used until the account is (back)filled.

Client list columns

The client index (/clients) shows Total invoiced and Total owing summary columns. A period filter (financial year, quarter, month, or custom range) controls which invoices are included in these totals. Both figures come from posted sales invoices on the client's party record.

Client detail page

Each client has a read-only detail page at /clients/{id}. It shows:

  • Client profile and billing defaults (editable inline)
  • Full paginated sales invoice history with amounts, statuses, and due dates
  • Running balance and total owing at the top
  • A link to the client's own receivable account transaction view

Access the detail page by clicking the View button on any row in the client list.

Permissions

  • parties-view-any / parties-view / parties-create / parties-update — to read and write the party record
  • party-relationships-create / party-relationships-update — to write the client relationship and its defaults
  • persons-create / persons-update — to add or edit contact people
